name: Western Union
description_short: Western Sun Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Oklahoma with competitive auto loans, checking/savings accounts, and certificates of deposit.
description_long:
Western Sun Federal Credit Union (WSFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union operating in Oklahoma with multiple branch locations including Broken Arrow (main office), Bixby, and Downtown Tulsa. The institution is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, serving the local community with personalized financial services. WSFCU was established to provide competitive rates and electronic financial services to its members in a friendly, professional manner.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of deposit and loan products. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (new vehicles starting at 5.49% APR), boat loans (starting at 7.64% APR), mortgage loans, and debt consolidation options. For savings and deposit accounts, WSFCU offers checking accounts with cash-back rewards (Kasasa Cash Back Checking with 3% cash back on debit purchases), savings accounts, money market accounts (up to 1.20% APY), and certificates of deposit (up to 3.46% APY). Members can apply for loans online or in person at any branch.

WFSCU distinguishes itself through competitive rate offerings, member-centric service, and local community involvement. The credit union actively engages with the community through events like Shred Days, car shows, electronics recycling programs, and charitable donations to local schools. Multiple branch locations with extended drive-through hours (7:30am-5:30pm weekdays, Saturday morning hours) provide convenient access. The institution emphasizes financial education through published articles on topics like credit scores, debt management, and safe shopping practices.

As a credit union, WSFCU operates on a not-for-profit basis, meaning earnings are returned to members through better rates rather than distributed to shareholders. However, membership eligibility may be restricted to certain communities or employment sectors depending on their field of membership requirements. The rates advertised are subject to change and credit approval, which is standard industry practice. For consumers seeking personalized service and community-focused banking with competitive rates on auto loans and savings products, WSFCU is a legitimate option in the Oklahoma market.

pros: Auto loan rates as low as 5.49% APR for new vehicles with online application option
Kasasa Cash Back Checking offering 3% cash back on debit card purchases up to $400 per cycle
Certificate of Deposit rates up to 3.46% APY, competitive for credit union products
Multiple branch locations in Broken Arrow, Bixby, and Downtown Tulsa with extended drive-through hours
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means earnings benefit members rather than external shareholders
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000
Active community involvement with free financial education resources and local events
cons: Membership eligibility may be restricted based on field of membership requirements (not open to all consumers)
Advertised rates are subject to change and require credit approval; actual rates may be higher
Limited geographic footprint compared to national banks, serving primarily Oklahoma locations
Website content does not clearly specify personal loan amounts, terms, or whether unsecured personal loans are available
No mention of online-only account options or digital banking features beyond online loan applications
best_for: Oklahoma residents seeking competitive auto loan rates with local branch service
Consumers eligible for credit union membership wanting cash-back rewards on debit card spending
Members looking for certificate of deposit products with above-average yields
Borrowers interested in debt consolidation loans with personalized service from a community-focused institution
diagnosis: Western Sun Federal Credit Union is best for Oklahoma residents who qualify for membership and seek competitive auto loan rates, cash-back checking rewards, and personalized service from a community-focused financial institution. The primary caveat is that credit union membership may have eligibility restrictions based on geographic location or employer affiliation, so prospective members should verify they qualify before applying.
services: Auto loans for new and used vehicles starting at 5.49% APR
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinancing
Kasasa Cash Back Checking with 3% rewards on debit purchases
Money Market accounts up to 1.20% APY
Certificates of Deposit up to 3.46% APY
Online banking and account login services
